properties.remark: REPORTS OF TREES AND LINES DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CARIBEL AND GLENWOOD IN KAMIAH.
PHOTO OF NUMEROUS GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL STONES IN WOODLAND SHARED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.

MOSTLY DIME SIZE HAIL BUT ESTIMATED HAIL UP TO THE SIZE OF QUARTERS. HAIL HAD JAGGED EDGES. PUT HOLES IN THE ROOF OF THE CHICKEN LOFT MADE OF HEAVY DUTY SEMI-CLEAR PLASTIC. WIPED OUT STRAWBERRIES AND OTHER PLANTS. MRMS ESTIMATED 0.3 TO 0.6 INCH MAX HAIL AT THIS LOCATION.

THIS REPORT IS A CORRECTION FROM THE EARLIER 1.75 INCH IN WOODLAND PROPER. FACEBOOK PHOTO WITH MEASURING TAPE REVEALED AT LEAST GOLF-BALL SIZED HAIL. THEY RECEIVED DENTS ON THEIR VEHICLE AND A CHICKEN DIED. MRMS MAX HAIL UNDER ESTIMATED BY HALF AN INCH. IT HAD 0.6 TO 1.2 ESTIMATIONS.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM WOODLAND. PHOTO OF NUMEROUS GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL STONES IN WOODLAND SHARED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
FACEBOOK REPORT SHOWED JAGGED HAIL STONE WITH A RULER POTENTIALLY MEASURING AROUND 2.1 INCHES. CAR WINDSHIELD WAS DAMAGED. TIME WAS ESTIMATED WHEN MRMS MAX HAIL SHOWED 1.11 INCHES.
TWITTER REPORT.
SPOTTER CALLED BACK AFTER FINDING HAIL OF 1 INCH.
FACEBOOK REPORT.
FACEBOOK REPORT. HAIL SIZE RANGED BETWEEN NICKEL SIZED TO HALF DOLLAR SIZED. SOME DENTS/SCRATCHES ON HER CAR.
LARGE ASPEN TREE WITH ESTIMATED 10 INCH DIAMETER FELL ON THE BACK OF A TRUCK CAUSING DAMAGE. TIME ESTIMATED.
A VERY LARGE FIR TREE, ESTIMATED 24 INCH BASE, WAS UPROOTED TO THE DUE EAST DIRECTION. TIME ESTIMATED FROM NEARBY OBSERVATIONS.
A LARGE, DEAD TREE WITH SIGNS OF ROT AT THE BASE TIPPED OVER AND HIT A BUILDING CAUSING SHINGLE AND ROOF DAMAGE.
LARGE WILLOW TREE BRANCHES 6 TO 10 INCHES IN DIAMETER WERE BROKEN OFF.

LARGE TREE BRANCHES, ESTIMATED DIAMETER OF 12 TO 18 INCHES WIDE, FELL DOWN OVER THE ENTRANCE TO BOETCHER PARK IN POLSON. TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED ON A NEARBY OBSERVED WIND GUST TO 49 MPH FROM A PRIVATE STATION.
REPORT RECEIVED VIA FACEBOOK MENTIONING QUARTER SIZE HAIL THAT RUINED THEIR GARDEN.
PICTURE WITH MEASUREMENT OF 1 INCH HAIL STONES.
CORRECTED FROM PREVIOUS LSR. MULTIPLE VIDEOS AND PHOTOS REVEALED POTENTIAL 70 MPH WIND COMBINED WITH ONE INCH OR BIGGER HAIL WITH SEVERAL TREES AND INSTANCES OF ROOF DAMAGE.
PICTURE SENT IN BY TWITTER.
SENT IN BY TWITTER.
STRONG WINDS CAUSING POWER OUTAGE AND A BROKEN COTTONWOOD TREE.

RECEIVED A CALL ABOUT A FALLEN TREE AND A BARN ROOF RIPPED OFF. THE CALLER STATED THAT HE EITHER HAD A VERY LOCALIZED MICROBURST OR A TORNADO. THE DAMAGE WAS VERY LOCALIZED AND THE ROOF WAS TWIRLING IN THE AIR, BUT HAD A HARD TIME SEEING THE STORM DUE TO THE RAIN.
SOCIAL MEDIA PHOTO. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR AND SOCIAL MEDIA POST. MRMS HAD 1.10 INCH MAX HAIL ESTIMATE. SIZE IS ESTIMATED. ONE HAILSTONE COULD HAVE BEEN AS BIG AS A HEN EGG.
LARGE QUANTITY OF NICKEL SIZE HAIL THAT CAUSED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO A GREENHOUSE BUSINESS. STRONG WINDS BROKE DECENT SIZE TREE BRANCHES, BUT UNABLE TO ESTIMATE WIND GUSTS.
REPORTS DAMAGE TO CARS IN THE NEIGHBORHOOD.
FACEBOOK HAIL PHOTO. COULD HAVE BEEN AS BIG AS A TENNIS BALL OR 2.50 INCHES.
REPORTS NUMEROUS BROKEN WINDSHIELDS.
FACEBOOK REPORT FROM EVERGREEN AREA. 40-50 MPH WINDS AND HEAVY RAIN ACCOMPANIED THE HAIL, WHICH RANGED BETWEEN GOLF BALL AND LIME SIZED.
HAIL SIZE RANGED BETWEEN PING PONGS AND GOLF BALLS.
MONTANA DOT IS REPORTING ROCKS ON THE ROAD.
SOCIAL MEDIA PHOTO.
FACEBOOK REPORT. HAIL RANGED FROM PEA SIZED UP TO 1.25 INCH IN DIAMETER. ALSO REPORTED A LIGHTNING STRIKE TO THEIR BARN.
PHOTO RETRIEVED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.
SOCIAL MEDIA PHOTO. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
POSSIBLE TOTAL LOSS AT CHERRY ORCHARD. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
PHOTO RETRIEVED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.
PHOTO RETRIEVED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.
PHOTO RETRIEVED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.
REPORT OF ROOF DAMAGE AT A BUSINESS IN TOWN.
REPORTS OF BLEACHER DAMAGE AND DAMAGE TO ONE OF THE MODULAR UNITS ON THE HIGH SCHOOL CAMPUS.
ROOF BLOWN OFF A TRAILER.
MULTIPLE WILLOW TREES, 6 INCHES IN DIAMETER, DOWNED BY THE WIND. THE STRONG WIND LASTED FOR A WHILE.
REPORT OF SEVERAL TREES AND TREE LIMBS DOWN. TWO REPORTS OF TREES ACROSS POWER LINES, CAUSING OUTAGES.
MESONET STATION SMYI1 SALMON.
MESONET STATION KRCI1 KRILEY CREEK RAWS.
WIND SENSOR ON THE WARM SPRINGS SETTLING POND.

MASSIVE HAIL DAMAGE INCLUDING ALMOST ALL THE WINDOWS IN THE HOUSE BROKEN POSSIBLY UP TO 8 TOTAL, A LARGE BRANCH BROKEN, BARN DOORS BROKEN POSSIBLY DUE TO HIGH WIND. A HEAVY SHOP DOOR WAS BLOWN OFF AND LANDED 60 FEET AWAY FROM THE SHOP. THE HAIL BUSTED THROUGH THE SIDING AND DENTED OTHER PARTS OF THE HOME. WHEAT CROPS WERE FLATTENED, POSSIBLY TENS OF THOUSANDS OF DOLLARS IN DAMAGE.
A WEATHER SPOTTER HEARD THAT MULTIPLE TREES WERE DOWN ON BEAVERSLIDE ROAD.
POWER OUTAGE THAT LASTED 23 HOURS IN THE GLENWOOD AREA. POWER POLES WERE REPORTEDLY DAMAGED IN THE AREA. TIME ESTIMATE BASED ON RADAR.
GUSTY WINDS WITH THUNDERSTORMS BROUGHT DOWN TREES IN THE AREA. ESTIMATED PEA SIZE HAIL.
HAIL BETWEEN QUARTER AND HALF DOLLAR SIZE REPORTED FROM SALMON RAPIDS LODGE. LOTS OF LEAVES DOWN BUT NO OTHER OBSERVED DAMAGE AT THIS TIME.
TREE WAS REPORTEDLY DOWN NEAR MILE MARKERS 143 AND 144 ON US-12.
PING PONG SIZE HAIL, HEAVY RAIN AND STRONG WINDS.
LANDSLIDE ON ROADWAY, ONE LANE BLOCKED. HIGHWAY 35 AT MILE MARKER 10.
HIGHWAY 35 CLOSED FROM MILE MARKER 6.1 TO 30.5 BECAUSE OF ROCK SLIDES.
